heads.refinement: per-face binary; label = rule_per_face > 0.5 (CIRCULAR — same OCC features the model ingests)

training.dataset: combined-v2: 500 parametric + 600 degraded + 293 featured (random fillet/chamfer) + 879 featured-degraded = 2272 graphs (2026-07-19)

val_in_distribution.note: source-grouped 80/20 (all variants of a base part stay on one side) -- leakage-free held-out. v2 val is HARDER than v1's (featured parts remove the smooth-prim shortcut); v1 numbers kept below for provenance.

heldout_fixtures.note: 27 hand-built fixtures / 3612 faces (labels_fixtures_v2); refinement head; circular label. v1 checkpoint on the SAME fixtures: acc 0.580 / recall 0.527. Fixture defect_acc is 0.148 for BOTH checkpoints - fixtures include deliberately-broken geometry labeled clean for lack of tags; not a usable defect metric.

real_cad_grabcad.note: 3 real STEPs, UNLABELED; pipeline outputs only; gate to expose generalization gap

real_cad_mcmaster.note: 12 real McMaster STEPs, UNLABELED presumed-clean (docs/validation/real_eval_v2.md, 2026-07-19). v2 checkpoint + hang guards: 11/12 analyzed (was 7/12), 0 errored. Defect head STILL false-positives on 11/11 (was 7/7) - fillet/chamfer augmentation on the box/cyl grammar did NOT close the real-CAD gap; root cause is NURBS/analytic surface types absent from the grammar itself.

provenance: weights/brepnet_meta.json (full per-epoch history), weights/eval_fixtures.json (fixtures eval). Regenerate: scripts/train.py + scripts/evaluate.py.
